brief_justification: Arrives at exactly 16.25 hours with correct per-phase arithmetic (10/8=1.25, 20/4=5, 10/1=10); goes one level beyond raw calculation by naming Amdahl's Law and Brooks's principle, but does not trace second-order effects like coordination overhead or why serial dominance grows; proceeds linearly with no reflection on assumptions or potential reasoning errors; no counterfactual check, boundary test, or re-derivation from a different angle is performed; uses 'minimum completion time' implicitly but never flags assumptions of perfect parallelism, zero coordination cost, or instantaneous handoff; considers only the stated allocation and never explores whether reassigning developers across phases could improve time; every step is explicit and labeled so a third party can fully reproduce the answer without external context.
